title: Fetal Brain Tissue Dissociation Protocol for Single-Cell RNA Sequencing
author: Siddharth Singh

steps.title: Collect fetal brains rapidly into ice-cold HBSS without Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺
steps.instruction: Collect fetal brains rapidly into ice-cold HBSS without Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺.

steps.title: Keep tissue cold and minimize the...
steps.instruction: Keep tissue cold and minimize the interval between dissection and enzyme exposure.

steps.title: Pool tissue to a total of...
steps.instruction: Pool tissue to a total of approximately 100 mg per sample/group if needed.

steps.title: Remove obvious blood contamination and gross non-neural debris before loading
steps.instruction: Remove obvious blood contamination and gross non-neural debris before loading.

steps.title: Proceed immediately to reagent loading
steps.instruction: Proceed immediately to reagent loading. Fetal brain is mechanically softer than neonatal/postnatal tissue and prolonged pre-digestion delays are more likely to increase debris and lower transcriptomic fidelity.

steps.title: Add 3 mL Buffer X to lyophilized Enzyme T
steps.instruction: Add 3 mL Buffer X to lyophilized Enzyme T.

steps.title: Close the vial and invert gently to mix
steps.instruction: Close the vial and invert gently to mix.

steps.title: Wait 5–10 min
steps.instruction: Wait 5–10 min.

steps.title: Do not pipette up and down
steps.instruction: Do not pipette up and down.

steps.title: Add 1 mL sterile water to lyophilized Enzyme A
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL sterile water to lyophilized Enzyme A.

steps.title: Do not vortex
steps.instruction: Do not vortex.

steps.title: Mix both reconstituted enzymes by gentle...
steps.instruction: Mix both reconstituted enzymes by gentle inversion before aliquoting or use.

steps.title: Prepare Enzyme mix 1
steps.instruction: Prepare Enzyme mix 1: 60 µL Enzyme T + 1890 µL Buffer X

steps.title: Prepare Enzyme mix 2
steps.instruction: Prepare Enzyme mix 2: 20 µL Buffer Y + 10 µL Enzyme A Use the full standard mix even though the tissue is below 400 mg.

steps.title: Add 1950 µL Enzyme mix 1...
steps.instruction: Add 1950 µL Enzyme mix 1 to one gentleMACS C Tube.

steps.title: Pre-heat the C Tube containing Enzyme...
steps.instruction: Pre-heat the C Tube containing Enzyme mix 1 at 37 °C for 10–15 min.

steps.title: Transfer the ~200 mg fetal brain...
steps.instruction: Transfer the ~200 mg fetal brain tissue into the C Tube.

steps.title: Close the tube tightly
steps.instruction: Close the tube tightly.

steps.title: Mount the C Tube upside down...
steps.instruction: Mount the C Tube upside down on the gentleMACS Dissociator sleeve.

steps.title: Ensure the tissue is positioned in the rotator/stator area
steps.instruction: Ensure the tissue is positioned in the rotator/stator area.

steps.title: Run the sequence exactly as follows:
steps.instruction: Run the sequence exactly as follows:

steps.title: Run m_brain_01
steps.instruction: Run m_brain_01.

steps.title: Incubate 15 min at 37 °C...
steps.instruction: Incubate 15 min at 37 °C under slow continuous rotation on the MACSmix Tube Rotator.

steps.title: Run m_brain_02
steps.instruction: Run m_brain_02.

steps.title: Add 30 µL Enzyme mix 2 to the C Tube
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL Enzyme mix 2 to the C Tube.

steps.title: Invert gently to mix
steps.instruction: Invert gently to mix.

steps.title: Do not vortex
steps.instruction: Do not vortex.

steps.title: Incubate 10 min at 37 °C under slow continuous rotation
steps.instruction: Incubate 10 min at 37 °C under slow continuous rotation.

steps.title: Run m_brain_03
steps.instruction: Run m_brain_03.

steps.title: Incubate another 10 min at 37...
steps.instruction: Incubate another 10 min at 37 °C under slow continuous rotation. Remove the tube and centrifuge briefly to collect the digest at the bottom.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: Low yield commonly results from tissue pieces sticking to the bottom or from insufficient agitation during incubation. Keep the tube rotating as instructed. If necessary, gently flick or invert the tube between incubations.

steps.title: Resuspend the digest gently after the final brief spin
steps.instruction: Resuspend the digest gently after the final brief spin.

steps.title: If the digest contains obvious flocculent...
steps.instruction: If the digest contains obvious flocculent debris or partially released tissue fragments, allow it to settle undisturbed for 30–60 s.

steps.title: Transfer the freer supernatant to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the freer supernatant to a clean tube, leaving the heaviest debris behind.

steps.title: Apply the suspension to a pre-wet...
steps.instruction: Apply the suspension to a pre-wet 70 µm SmartStrainer on a 50 mL tube.

steps.title: Wash through the strainer with 10 mL HBSS with Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺
steps.instruction: Wash through the strainer with 10 mL HBSS with Ca²⁺/Mg²⁺.

steps.title: Discard the strainer
steps.instruction: Discard the strainer.

steps.title: Centrifuge the filtrate at 300 ×...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the filtrate at 300 × g for 10 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant completely
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant completely.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet gently in an...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet gently in an appropriate volume of cold buffer.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: The 10x embryonic neural tissue workflow includes a 1 min debris-settling step before transferring the cell-rich supernatant, followed by a final strain to remove residual debris. That logic is useful for the fetal brain because fetal tissue releases cells easily, but can also generate fine debris that interferes with counting and downstream microfluidics.

steps.title: If visible micro-aggregates remain after the first cleanup:
steps.instruction: If visible micro-aggregates remain after the first cleanup:

steps.title: Pass the suspension through a 30...
steps.instruction: Pass the suspension through a 30 µm strainer immediately before counting. Use this only when aggregate removal is more important than maximal recovery of larger cells, because smaller filters increase cell loss.

steps.title: Do not fix until all of the following are true:
steps.instruction: Do not fix until all of the following are true:

steps.title: The suspension is visibly single-cell
steps.instruction: The suspension is visibly single-cell.

steps.title: It is not stringy
steps.instruction: It is not stringy.

steps.title: It contains minimal clumps and minimal residual tissue fragments
steps.instruction: It contains minimal clumps and minimal residual tissue fragments.

steps.title: Cell concentration is known
steps.instruction: Cell concentration is known.

steps.title: Viability is acceptable
steps.instruction: Viability is acceptable.

steps.title: Use the following QC approach
steps.instruction: Use the following QC approach: Target >80% viability, ideally >90%.

steps.title: For fetal-brain suspensions with substantial debris,...
steps.instruction: For fetal-brain suspensions with substantial debris, use fluorescence-based viability counting or manual hemocytometer confirmation rather than trypan blue alone.

steps.title: Note
steps.instruction: Note: For fixed cells, isolated nuclei, or suspensions with high debris levels, fluorescent viability dyes are strongly recommended; trypan blue can overestimate counts by including stained debris.

steps.title: Use this only if the sample is clearly under-dissociated
steps.instruction: Use this only if the sample is clearly under-dissociated.

steps.title: Option A Add 30 µL Enzyme...
steps.instruction: Option A Add 30 µL Enzyme mix 2 per mL of cell suspension

steps.title: Mix gently
steps.instruction: Mix gently

steps.title: Incubate at least
steps.instruction: Incubate at least

steps.title: min at 37 °C under slow continuous rotation
steps.instruction: min at 37 °C under slow continuous rotation

steps.title: Option B For sticky threads/particles, add...
steps.instruction: Option B For sticky threads/particles, add 30 µL Enzyme mix 2 per 2 mL

steps.title: Incubate 5–10 min at 37 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate 5–10 min at 37 °C

steps.title: Re-filter and re-check the sample
steps.instruction: Re-filter and re-check the sample

steps.title: Begin fixation only after a homogeneous...
steps.instruction: Begin fixation only after a homogeneous single-cell suspension has been obtained.

steps.title: Transfer the cells into a Falcon...
steps.instruction: Transfer the cells into a Falcon tube containing 5–10 mL cold PBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 500 g, 4 °C, 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 500 g, 4 °C, 5 min.

steps.title: Carefully remove the PBS
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the PBS.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL cold 1X PBS
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 1 mL cold 1X PBS.

steps.title: Tap gently to mix and place...
steps.instruction: Tap gently to mix and place the tube on ice immediately.

steps.title: While the tube is on
steps.instruction: While the tube is on

steps.title: slow vortex, add 4 mL ice-cold methanol dropwise
steps.instruction: slow vortex, add 4 mL ice-cold methanol dropwise.

steps.title: Incubate the PBS:methanol suspension on ice...
steps.instruction: Incubate the PBS:methanol suspension on ice for 20 min, tapping occasionally.

steps.title: Aliquot into 1
steps.instruction: Aliquot into 1.5 mL tubes, typically 1 mL per tube.

steps.title: Target 1–2 million cells per mL
steps.instruction: Target 1–2 million cells per mL.

steps.title: Store at −80 °C
steps.instruction: Store at −80 °C.

steps.title: Transport on dry ice for downstream transcriptomics processing
steps.instruction: Transport on dry ice for downstream transcriptomics processing. Note: The methanol must be added dropwise during slow vortexing. Failure to do so leads to clumping and can render the sample unsuitable for single-cell RNA-seq.

steps.title: Store fixed aliquots at −80 °C
steps.instruction: Store fixed aliquots at −80 °C.

steps.title: Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles
steps.instruction: Avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles.

steps.title: Ship or transfer on dry ice
steps.instruction: Ship or transfer on dry ice.

steps.title: Record sample ID, tissue source, pooled...
steps.instruction: Record sample ID, tissue source, pooled mass, digestion date, operator, kit lot, and estimated cell concentration before fixation.
